required_met: 2+ years of experience in technology (SaaS environment via Django/RESTful APIs, Lexicon training, and Dell technical support)
Strong written and verbal communication skills (multilingual, awards for customer satisfaction, teaching/translation experience)
Analytical and detail-oriented (mathematics background, database/SQL expertise, machine learning coursework)
Proven ability to provide expert advice (Dell technical support, customer service awards)
Self-starter and team player (agile project course, Caritas Norge coordination)
High computer literacy and ability to learn new software (Python/Django, Java, Flutter, CRM readiness inferred)
Proactive and dependable (awards, consistent career progression)
Problem solver with intellectual curiosity (machine learning, software design, technical troubleshooting)
required_missing: 2+ years of project management experience in a Customer Success role (current experience is technical training, not CSM-specific)
Direct experience with CRM software (Salesforce/Gainsight not explicitly mentioned)
Explicit higher education industry knowledge (though transferable from Dell/tech support)
preferred_met: Public speaking/webinar leadership (teacher assistant, Dell training implied)
Multi-tasking and priority adjustment (Dell technical support, refugee coordination)
match.logistics_notes: Role requires US work authorization; candidate is based in Sweden with no mention of eligibility.
match.cultural_fit_summary: Strong alignment with Ellucian’s collaborative, customer-centric culture. Demonstrated empathy (refugee support), adaptability (multilingual, cross-cultural roles), and passion for education (teaching, lifelong learning). Technical curiosity and problem-solving mindset fit the innovative, data-driven environment.
match.reasoning: Candidate meets core technical and soft skill requirements but lacks direct Customer Success Manager experience. Transferable skills from technical support, teaching, and project-based training partially offset this gap. Logistics (US work authorization) are the primary barrier, though cultural fit and analytical strengths are compelling for review.
